methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Cobod
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Material Evolution
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Inversion Space is a Series A-stage company in Housing & Construction that has raised $44M to date. The broader sector comprises three identified private peers (ICON, Cobod, Material Evolution) and no direct public comparables, limiting traditional peer-median benchmarking.

Valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, the only applicable triangulation method given data constraints. This approach applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to the next financing stage, anchored to the company's $44M total raised as a proxy for current implied valuation. This yields an implied value of $88M. The stage-progression method reflects expected value creation between fundraising rounds but does not account for sector-specific growth patterns or the company's relative positioning within the Housing & Construction universe.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $75M to $101M, with a median of $88M. Key uncertainties stem from the narrow peer set; no same-stage or adjacent-stage Housing & Construction comparables met minimum thresholds (≥3 datapoints) for reliable peer-median cross-validation. Revenue multiples, growth rates, and unit economics specific to Inversion Space's business model remain unmeasured against the identified peer set. Further diligence on financial performance and market traction would materially refine this range.
